title: Aegean numerals
body: Aegean numerals are an additive sign-value numeral system that was used by the Minoan and Mycenaean civilizations. They are attested in the Linear A and Linear B scripts. They may have also survived in the Cypro-Minoan script, where a single sign with the value "100" is attested on a large clay tablet from Enkomi.
categories: Aegean languages in the Bronze Age
